title: systemd services that run containers using sdnotify restart every 90s due to timeout error
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

After upgrade from 2.2.0 to 2.2.1, I get (timeout) error on all (6) different services running with systemd and sdnotify, that in turn makes all services to restart every ~90s for no apparent reason. 

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. create non-root user `sudo useradd -u 1500 --create-home testuser; sudo loginctl enable-linger testuser`
2. create following service config at `/etc/systemd/system/test.service`
```
[Unit]
Description=Podman test.service
Wants=network.target
After=network-online.target

[Service]
Environment=PODMAN_SYSTEMD_UNIT=%N
Environment=XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/run/user/1500
User=testuser
Group=testuser
Restart=always
ExecStartPre=-/usr/bin/podman rm --ignore -f test
ExecStart=/usr/bin/podman run --log-level=debug --sdnotify=conmon \
      --name test \
      busybox:latest \
      sh -c "while true; do $(echo date); sleep 1; done"
ExecStop=/usr/bin/podman stop -t 10 test
ExecStopPost=-/usr/bin/podman rm --ignore -f test

Type=notify
KillMode=none
NotifyAccess=all

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
```
3. start the service `sudo systemctl start test`

**Describe the results you received:**
`sudo systemctl start test` - command never success, eventually throws timeout error  
`sudo -H -u testuser bash -c "/usr/bin/podman logs -f test"` - looks like the container is working correctly for ~90 seconds and then it restarts

Logs are also not showing anything in particular, the services work normally for ~90s, yet all are stuck in `activating (start)` stage, before being restarted with following error:
```
Dec 14 18:41:21 ethnode podman[7604]: time="2020-12-14T18:41:21Z" level=debug msg="Attaching to container f13b64deaf8a1c0501c3bbdb7614588cf20199857d07700340afe405dee535d9"
Dec 14 18:41:21 ethnode podman[7604]: time="2020-12-14T18:41:21Z" level=debug msg="connecting to socket /run/user/1500/libpod/tmp/socket/f13b64deaf8a1c0501c3bbdb7614588cf20199857d07700340afe405dee535d9/attach"
Dec 14 18:41:21 ethnode podman[7604]: time="2020-12-14T18:41:21Z" level=debug msg="Starting container f13b64deaf8a1c0501c3bbdb7614588cf20199857d07700340afe405dee535d9 with command [sh -c while sleep 1; do echo \"Hi\"; done]"
Dec 14 18:41:21 ethnode podman[7604]: time="2020-12-14T18:41:21Z" level=debug msg="Started container f13b64deaf8a1c0501c3bbdb7614588cf20199857d07700340afe405dee535d9"
Dec 14 18:41:21 ethnode podman[7604]: time="2020-12-14T18:41:21Z" level=debug msg="Notify sent successfully"
Dec 14 18:41:21 ethnode podman[7604]: 2020-12-14 18:41:21.849449862 +0000 UTC m=+0.128763713 container start f13b64deaf8a1c0501c3bbdb7614588cf20199857d07700340afe405dee535d9 (image=docker.io/library/busybox:latest, name=test, PODMAN_SYSTEMD_UNIT=test)
Dec 14 18:41:21 ethnode podman[7604]: 2020-12-14 18:41:21.849550543 +0000 UTC m=+0.128864413 container attach f13b64deaf8a1c0501c3bbdb7614588cf20199857d07700340afe405dee535d9 (image=docker.io/library/busybox:latest, name=test, PODMAN_SYSTEMD_UNIT=test)
Dec 14 18:41:21 ethnode podman[7604]: time="2020-12-14T18:41:21Z" level=debug msg="Enabling signal proxying"
Dec 14 18:42:51 ethnode systemd[1]: test.service: start operation timed out. Terminating.
Dec 14 18:43:02 ethnode systemd[1]: test.service: Main process exited, code=exited, status=137/n/a
Dec 14 18:43:02 ethnode systemd[1]: test.service: Failed with result 'timeout'.
Dec 14 18:43:02 ethnode systemd[1]: Failed to start Podman test.service.
Dec 14 18:43:02 ethnode systemd[1]: test.service: Scheduled restart job, restart counter is at 7.
```
similar from prometheus.service:
```
Dec 14 16:27:05 ethnode podman[18730]: level=info ts=2020-12-14T16:27:05.617Z caller=main.go:694 msg="Server is ready to receive web requests."
Dec 14 16:28:35 ethnode systemd[1]: prometheus.service: start operation timed out. Terminating.
Dec 14 16:28:35 ethnode systemd[1]: prometheus.service: Failed with result 'timeout'.
Dec 14 16:28:35 ethnode systemd[1]: Failed to start Podman prometheus.service.
Dec 14 16:28:35 ethnode systemd[1]: prometheus.service: Scheduled restart job, restart counter is at 1.
Dec 14 16:28:35 ethnode systemd[1]: Stopped Podman prometheus.service.
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
Container should not restart, and the systemd status of the service should be `active` instead of `activating`

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
This happened today after routine upgrade of packages including upgrade of podman from 2.2.0 -> 2.2.1

**Output of `podman version`:**
`sudo -H -u testuser bash -c "/usr/bin/podman version"`
```
Version:      2.2.1
API Version:  2.1.0
Go Version:   go1.15.2
Built:        Thu Jan  1 00:00:00 1970
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**
`sudo -H -u testuser bash -c "/usr/bin/podman info --debug"`
```
host:
  arch: amd64
  buildahVersion: 1.18.0
  cgroupManager: cgroupfs
  cgroupVersion: v1
  conmon:
    package: 'conmon: /usr/libexec/podman/conmon'
    path: /usr/libexec/podman/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 2.0.20, commit: '
  cpus: 8
  distribution:
    distribution: ubuntu
    version: "20.04"
  eventLogger: journald
  hostname: ethnode
  idMappings:
    gidmap:
    - container_id: 0
      host_id: 1500
      size: 1
    - container_id: 1
      host_id: 689824
      size: 65536
    uidmap:
    - container_id: 0
      host_id: 1500
      size: 1
    - container_id: 1
      host_id: 689824
      size: 65536
  kernel: 5.4.0-58-generic
  linkmode: dynamic
  memFree: 12943216640
  memTotal: 33539588096
  ociRuntime:
    name: runc
    package: 'containerd.io: /usr/bin/runc'
    path: /usr/bin/runc
    version: |-
      runc version 1.0.0-rc92
      commit: ff819c7e9184c13b7c2607fe6c30ae19403a7aff
      spec: 1.0.2-dev
  os: linux
  remoteSocket:
    path: /run/user/1500/podman/podman.sock
  rootless: true
  slirp4netns:
    executable: /usr/bin/slirp4netns
    package: 'slirp4netns: /usr/bin/slirp4netns'
    version: |-
      slirp4netns version 1.1.7
      commit: unknown
      libslirp: 4.3.1-git
      SLIRP_CONFIG_VERSION_MAX: 3
      libseccomp: 2.4.3
  swapFree: 8589930496
  swapTotal: 8589930496
  uptime: 1h 51m 59.19s (Approximately 0.04 days)
registries:
  search:
  - docker.io
  - quay.io
store:
  configFile: /home/testuser/.config/containers/storage.conf
  containerStore:
    number: 1
    paused: 0
    running: 1
    stopped: 0
  graphDriverName: vfs
  graphOptions: {}
  graphRoot: /home/testuser/.local/share/containers/storage
  graphStatus: {}
  imageStore:
    number: 1
  runRoot: /run/user/1500/containers
  volumePath: /home/testuser/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes
version:
  APIVersion: 2.1.0

```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
podman/unknown,now 2.2.1~4 amd64 [installed]
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body:
From info:
`    version: 'conmon version 2.0.20, commit: '`

I think we need 2.0.21 minimum for this?

Tried with building and replacing  `/usr/libexec/podman/conmon` with never binaries and the issue remained the same for:
```
  conmon:
    package: 'conmon: /usr/libexec/podman/conmon'
    path: /usr/libexec/podman/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 2.0.22-dev, commit: b1c51875faf6d129fd32085bb44e810d96493402'
```
and
```
  conmon:
    package: 'conmon: /usr/libexec/podman/conmon'
    path: /usr/libexec/podman/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 2.0.21, commit: 35a2fa83022e56e18af7e6a865ba5d7165fa2a4a'
```
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
I think we now require an updated conmon to deal with sdnotify
yeah the earliest version that supports SDNOTIFY is 2.0.22

rpm -q conmon
conmon-2.0.25-1.fc33.x86_64

Looks like this is available with podman  3.0.

I'm having the same issue on podman 3.2.2 and conmon 2.0.29 **for rootless mode** (see the `User=` and `Group=` lines).
My systemd service file looks like this:
```
[Unit]
Description=Podman container-nginxtest.service
Wants=network.target
After=network-online.target
RequiresMountsFor=%t/containers

[Service]
Environment=PODMAN_SYSTEMD_UNIT=%n
Restart=on-failure
ExecStart=/usr/bin/podman run --log-level=debug --sdnotify=conmon --cgroups=no-conmon --replace --network=private --name nginxtest --restart always --pull never nginx
ExecStop=/usr/bin/podman stop --ignore -t 240 nginx
ExecStopPost=-/usr/bin/podman rm --ignore -f nginx
Type=notify
NotifyAccess=all
User=nginx
Group=nginx
WorkingDirectory=/srv/nginx

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target default.target
```
I tried with `--cgroups=no-conmon` and without with the same result.\
The log has these lines:
```
level=info msg="Got Conmon PID as 1644"
level=debug msg="Notify MAINPID sent successfully"
[...]
level=debug msg="Notify sent successfully"
```
Systemd does not get the notification and restarts the service.\
When I remove the `User=` and `Group=` lines it works as expected:
```
level=info msg="Got Conmon PID as 2474"
level=debug msg="Notify MAINPID sent successfully"
[...]
level=debug msg="Notify sent successfully"
systemd[1]: Started Podman container-nginxtest.service.
```
In the second case everything works fine.

Edit: On a systemd user instance it also works as expected. I would prefer the system instance though because I have some dependencies that cannot be run by the user and I want to avoid setuid/sudo.

Edit2: Seems to be related to https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/10677

*Please forgive my cross-posting. I'm trying really hard not to create a potential duplicate issue but it seems there are several relevant issues already present. My prior post was in #10677. To say the least, it seems to me that both this issue and 10677 were hastily closed without validating things worked beforehand.*

I have a similar experience as @tobwen, @bytniak and @flo-at when attempting to run a podman container as a systemd service with `Type=notify` and using the `--sdnotify=conmon` option on the run command. However, the complaint from systemd when attempting to start the service unit is a little different. Also tried `--cgroups=split` just in case there was some issue there but the results were identical.

podman-dwspectrum.service:
```
[Unit]
Description=Podman for Digital Watchdog Spectrum
Wants=network.target
After=network-online.target

[Service]
WorkingDirectory=/tank/enc/dwspectrum
User=dwspectrum
Group=dwspectrum
ExecStartPre=/usr/bin/rm -f %T/%N.cid
ExecStartPre=/usr/bin/podman rm --ignore -f dwspectrum
ExecStart=/usr/bin/podman run --cidfile %T/%N.cid  --cgroups=no-conmon \
  --sdnotify=conmon \
  --detach \
  --pull=always \
  --log-driver=journald \
  --log-opt tag=dwspectrum \
  --name=dwspectrum \
  --env TZ=Americas/Detroit \
  --hostname=watchdog \
  --dns-search=subly.me \
  --publish 7001:7001 \
  --volume /tank/enc/dwspectrum/config:/config \
  --volume /tank/enc/dwspectrum/media:/media \
  --volume /tank/enc/dwspectrum/archive:/archive \
  docker.io/ptr727/dwspectrum-lsio:4.2.0.32660
ExecStop=/usr/bin/podman stop --ignore dwspectrum -t 10
ExecStopPost=/usr/bin/podman rm --ignore -f dwspectrum
ExecStopPost=/usr/bin/rm -f %T/%N.cid
TimeoutStopSec=3m
Type=notify
NotifyAccess=all

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target default.target
```

systemd output when starting unit fails:
```
jon@arc:/etc/systemd/system$ sudo systemctl start podman-dwspectrum
Job for podman-dwspectrum.service failed because the service did not take the steps required by its unit configuration.
See "systemctl status podman-dwspectrum.service" and "journalctl -xe" for details.
```

journal excerpt from systemd status:
```
Aug 07 23:01:59 arc systemd[1]: Starting Podman for Digital Watchdog Spectrum...
Aug 07 23:02:04 arc systemd[1]: podman-dwspectrum.service: Failed with result 'protocol'.
Aug 07 23:02:04 arc systemd[1]: Failed to start Podman for Digital Watchdog Spectrum.
```

When doing `pgrep -au dwspectrum` after starting the service as `Type=notify` it's very clear that the container isn't starting properly. I don't see any conmon process or others that show up if I run the container as a `Type=exec` or `Type=simple` service unit. And, I suppose I should clarify that this container service works just fine as a simple or exec type unit. I'm just attempting to try the systemd notify integration that podman purportedly has. 

I am unclear if I'm either misusing the `--sdnotify=conmon` option or if it simply isn't doing what the documentation suggests it should (conmon sending systemd a "ready" notification). This is on podman 3.0.1 on Debian testing.
